title: Find a shorter side using Pythagoras' theorem
problem: A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $13$ cm and one leg of $5$ cm. Find the length of the other leg.

steps.instruction: Substitute the known values. The hypotenuse $c = 13$ and one leg $b = 5$.
Take the square root to find $a$.

title: Find the height a ladder reaches
problem: A 10 m ladder leans against a wall. The foot of the ladder is 4 m from the base of the wall. Find the height the ladder reaches up the wall, correct to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Identify the sides. The ladder is the hypotenuse ($c = 10$ m), the horizontal distance is one leg ($b = 4$ m), and the height $h$ is the unknown leg.
Take the square root and round to 2 decimal places.

title: Find the height of an isosceles triangle
problem: An isosceles triangle has two equal sides of $13$ cm and a base of $10$ cm. Find the perpendicular height of the triangle.

steps.instruction: Draw a perpendicular from the apex to the base. It bisects the base into two equal halves.
This creates a right-angled triangle with hypotenuse $13$ cm (the equal side) and one leg $5$ cm (the half-base). Write Pythagoras' theorem.
